zh.Novgorod, Velikiy, Novgorodskaya Oblast': 俄羅斯Новгородская ОбластьВеликий Новгород諾福格洛德
zh.lifeSummary: 當Roman “the Grand” Mstislavich Rurik於1153年出生在俄羅斯Новгородская ОбластьВеликий Новгород諾福格洛德時，他的父親Mścisław II Izjasławicz Rurykowicz21歲，母親Agnieszka of Poland16歲。他於1176年與Maria Kotjanova Yakunovna Princess of Novgorod And Polowzes在俄羅斯Новгородская ОбластьВеликий Новгород諾福格洛德諾福格洛德結婚。他去世於1228年在烏克蘭ВолинськаЛуцький районТорчин享年75歲，埋葬在烏克蘭Київ。

zh-hans.Novgorod, Velikiy, Novgorodskaya Oblast': 俄罗斯Новгородская ОбластьВеликий Новгород諾福格洛德
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Roman “the Grand” Mstislavich Rurik于1153年出生在俄罗斯Новгородская ОбластьВеликий Новгород諾福格洛德时，他的父亲Mścisław II Izjasławicz Rurykowicz21岁，母亲Agnieszka of Poland16岁。他于1176年与Maria Kotjanova Yakunovna Princess of Novgorod And Polowzes在俄罗斯Новгородская ОбластьВеликий Новгород諾福格洛德諾福格洛德结婚。他去世于1228年在乌克兰ВолинськаЛуцький районТорчин享年75岁，埋葬在乌克兰Київ。

nameMeanings.name: Rurik
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Slovak (mainly Jurík): from a pet form of the personal name Juraj, an equivalent of Latin Georgius (see George ).

variants.name: George
meanings: English, Welsh, French, and Romanian: from the personal name George, Latin Georgius, Greek Geōrgios, from an adjectival form, geōrgios ‘rustic’, of Greek geōrgos ‘farmer’. This became established as a personal name in classical times through its association with the fashion for pastoral poetry. Its popularity in western Europe increased at the time of the Crusades, which brought greater contact with the Orthodox Church, in which several Christian martyrs and saints of this name are venerated, in particular a saint believed to have been martyred at Nicomedia in AD 303, who, however, is at best a shadowy figure historically. Nevertheless, by the end of the Middle Ages Saint George had become associated with an unhistorical legend of dragon-slaying exploits, which caught the popular imagination throughout Europe, and he came to be considered the patron saint of England among other places. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed many cognates from other languages, e.g. German Georg , Assyrian/Chaldean Giwargis, Gewargis , or Georgis , and Albanian Gjergji , and also their patronymics and other derivatives, e.g. Greek Georgiadis , Georgopoulos , Hatzigeorgiou ‘George the Pilgrim’, and Papageorgiou , Romanian Georgescu or Gheorghescu, Serbian Djordjevic . The name George is also found among Christians in southern India (compare Geevarghese and Varghese ), but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US.
Native American (e.g. Navajo): adoption of the English personal name George (see 1 above) as a surname.

variants.name: Jurick
meanings: Germanized or Americanized form of Slovak Jurík (see Jurik ).
Americanized form of Croatian Jurić, Slovenian and Slovak Jurič (see Juric ). Compare Yurick .

nameMeanings.name: Roman
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: , Polish, and Czech: from the Late Latin personal name Romanus, originally an ethnic name meaning ‘Roman’ (a derivative of Roma; compare Romolo ). This name was borne by a large number of early saints, and in the 10th century was given as a baptismal name to Boris, son of Vladimir, the ruler who Christianized Kievan Russia . Boris and his brother Gleb were murdered by their brother Svyatopolk and canonized as martyrs. Use of the name in the English-speaking world is recent, influenced perhaps by the film director Roman Polanski ( b. 1933 as Raimund Liebling ).

nameMeanings.name: “the
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: from a short form of an ancient Germanic personal name formed with odal ‘inherited property’ as the first element.

nameMeanings.name: Grand”
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: probably a variant of   Grundy  .

variants.name: Grundy
meanings: English (Lancashire): from the Middle English personal name Grondi, Grundi, which is probably a metathesized form of Old French Gondri (ancient Germanic Gundric). See Gundry .

en.lifeSummary: When Roman “the Grand” Mstislavich Rurik was born in 1153, in Novgorod, velikiy novgorod, Novgorod Oblast, Russia, his father, Mścisław II Izjasławicz Rurykowicz, was 21 and his mother, Agnieszka of Poland, was 16. He married Maria Kotjanova Yakunovna Princess of Novgorod And Polowzes in 1176, in Novgorod, Novgorod, velikiy novgorod, Novgorod Oblast, Russia. He died in 1228, in Torchyn, Lutsk, Volyn, Ukraine, at the age of 75, and was buried in Kyiv, Ukraine.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Roman “the Grand” Mstislavich Rurik est né en 1153, à Novgorod, velikiy novgorod, Novgorodskaya Oblast', Russie, son père, Mścisław II Izjasławicz Rurykowicz, avait 21 ans et sa mère, Agnieszka of Poland, avait 16 ans. Il a épousé Maria Kotjanova Yakunovna Princess of Novgorod And Polowzes en 1176, à Novgorod, Novgorod, velikiy novgorod, Novgorodskaya Oblast', Russie. Il est décédé en 1228, à Torchyn, Lutsʹkyy Rayon, Volyn, Ukraine, à l’âge de 75 ans, et a été enterré à Kyiv, Ukraine.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Roman “the Grand” Mstislavich Rurik родился в 1153 г. в местности новгород, Великий Новгород, Новгородская Область, Россия, его отцу по имени Mścisław II Izjasławicz Rurykowicz было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Agnieszka of Poland, 16. Он женился на женщине по имени Maria Kotjanova Yakunovna Princess of Novgorod And Polowzes в 1176 г. в местности новгород, новгород, Великий Новгород, Новгородская Область, Россия. Он умер в 1228 г. в местности Торчин, Луцкий район, Волынская, Украина в возрасте 75 лет и был похоронен в местности Киев, Украина.
